description: secretx-file/src/lib.rs:78 uses std::fs::read which pre-sizes via metadata().len() but falls back to growing the Vec when len is 0 or unknown (e.g., procfs files, named pipes, character devices, sysfs). Each grow doubles capacity and the old allocation is freed without zeroization — secret bytes from the partial read remain in the heap until overwritten by another allocator request. SecretValue's Zeroizing wrapper protects only the FINAL Vec. Trigger: secretx:file:/proc/self/cmdline or any pseudo-file with metadata.len()==0 reporting actual content. Fix: use File::open + metadata + read into a Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>::with_capacity(size), or document that file sources must be regular files with accurate size.
title: [bug] std::fs::read may grow Vec, leaving secret bytes in deallocated buffers (no zeroize)
